Fish is a must because it means having plenty every year. After dinner we watch the Spring Festival Gala on television. We talk, laugh, and eat snacks until midnight. When the clock strikes twelve, we hear firecrackers and see fireworks light up the sky. 🔊

On New Year's Day, I wake up early and put on my new clothes. I say "Happy New Year" to my parents and grandparents. They give me red envelopes with lucky money inside. This is my favorite part of the festival. Then we visit our relatives and friends to share good wishes. 🔊

The celebration lasts for many days. We eat and play and enjoy the holiday break from school. Spring Festival is about family, love, and hope. I always feel warm and happy during this special time of year. 🔊

Mid-Autumn Festival comes in September or October when the moon is at its fullest and brightest. It is a time for family reunions. On this day, my family and I always get together at my grandparents' home. My aunts, uncles, and cousins come too. The house is full of laughter and happy voices. 🔊

In the evening, we have a special dinner together. We eat dishes made with seasonal foods like crab, pumpkin, and lotus root. After dinner, we go outside to look at the moon. My grandfather tells us the story of Chang'e, the lady who lives on the moon, and her rabbit companion. I have heard the story many times, but I still enjoy it every year. 🔊

Then we eat mooncakes. Mooncakes are round and sweet, just like the full moon. Some have red bean paste inside, and others have egg yolk. My favorite is the one with lotus seed paste. My grandmother always buys a big box with different flavors so everyone can choose what they like. 🔊

Looking at the bright moon, I think about people who cannot be with their families on this day. The festival reminds me to be thankful for the people I love. The round moon and round mooncakes stand for togetherness and completeness. It is a beautiful tradition. 🔊

Christmas is not a traditional Chinese holiday, but many young people enjoy celebrating it. Last year my friends and I decided to have a small Christmas party at my house. We spent a whole afternoon decorating. We put up a small plastic tree and hung colorful lights around the living room. 🔊

On Christmas Eve, my friends arrived one by one. Each person brought a small gift wrapped in bright paper. We put all the gifts under the tree. We played Christmas songs on the speaker and sang along. Some of us knew the English words, and some just hummed the tune. We also watched a funny Christmas movie and ate popcorn. 🔊

The best part was the gift exchange. We wrote numbers on pieces of paper and put them in a hat. Each person picked a number and took the matching gift. I got a soft green scarf from my best friend Mei. I gave a small notebook with a cat on the cover to another friend. 🔊

Although Christmas is a Western holiday, the spirit of giving and sharing with friends is something anyone can enjoy. That evening I felt close to my friends. We promised to do it again next year. For me, Christmas has become a time to show my friends I care. 🔊

Children's Day is on June the first, and it is one of my favorite days of the year. On that day, there is no school. Instead, my school holds a big celebration. The whole campus is decorated with balloons and colorful flags. Everyone comes in their best clothes, and there is an exciting feeling in the air. 🔊

The day starts with a short show. Students sing songs, dance, and perform short plays on the school stage. Last year my class did a traditional Chinese dance. We practiced for weeks before the big day. When we finished, the crowd clapped loudly. I felt so proud. After the show, the principal gives a speech and wishes all the students a happy Children's Day. 🔊

Then the fun really begins. The school sets up game booths all over the playground. There are ring toss games, guessing games, and relay races. Students can win small prizes like pencils, erasers, and candies. I tried almost every game and won a cute little notebook. We ran from booth to booth, laughing and cheering. 🔊

In the afternoon, I go home and my parents usually have a small surprise for me. Sometimes it is a new book, sometimes a toy, and sometimes we go out for ice cream. Children's Day makes me feel special and loved. I am already looking forward to the next one. 🔊

Every spring our school holds an art festival. It lasts for three days and is one of the biggest events of the year. Students from all grades take part. There are singing competitions, painting shows, handwriting displays, and drama performances. The campus becomes a colorful and lively place. 🔊

My favorite part is the painting and calligraphy show. The school hallway is turned into a gallery. Paintings by students are hung on the walls. Some are watercolors, some are oil paintings, and some are simple pencil sketches. I like to walk slowly and look at each piece. Last year my painting of a sunset river won third prize. 🔊

The singing competition is also very popular. Students sing alone or in groups on the big stage in the school hall. The lights shine brightly and the music fills the room. I am too shy to sing in front of so many people, but I love to sit in the audience and listen. 🔊

The art festival brings the whole school together. It lets us show our talents and appreciate the talents of others. It also gives us a break from our usual studies. I think every school should have an art festival. It helps us grow in ways that books alone cannot. 🔊

Everyone makes mistakes. I have made my share, and one of them taught me how important it is to say sorry. It happened last year with my best friend, Zhang Wei. We had been friends since primary school and did almost everything together. 🔊

One day we had a big argument over something small. We were working on a group project, and I thought he was not doing his part. I got angry and said some unkind words. He got angry too. We did not speak to each other for three days after that. 🔊

Those three days were very hard. I missed talking and laughing with him. I knew I should apologize, but I was too proud at first. My mother noticed I was quiet and asked what was wrong. When I told her, she said, "A true friend knows how to say sorry. It takes more courage to apologize than to stay angry." 🔊

On the fourth day, I walked up to Zhang Wei after class. I said, "I am sorry for what I said. It was wrong. Can we be friends again?" He looked at me and smiled. "I am sorry too," he said. We shook hands and everything was okay again. I learned that saying sorry does not make you weak. It makes you a better person and a better friend. 🔊

A gift does not have to be big or expensive to be special. The best gift I ever received was small and simple, but it meant the world to me. It was a handmade bookmark from my little cousin, Xiaomei. 🔊

It was my birthday, and my family had a small party at home. There was a cake, some snacks, and a few balloons. Many relatives came, and everyone brought gifts. I got books, clothes, and a new pair of shoes. I was happy with all of them. Then Xiaomei came up to me with something in her small hands. 🔊

She handed me a rectangle of thick paper. On it, she had drawn a sun, some flowers, and a rainbow with colored pencils. The drawing was not perfect. Across the top, she had written "Happy Brithday" with a missing letter. She had added a ribbon at the top to make it a bookmark. 🔊

I almost cried when I saw it. She had spent a long time making it, giving me her time and her love. No store could sell something like that. Even now, I still use that bookmark. Every time I see it, I remember her sweet face and the lesson she taught me: the best gifts come from the heart. 🔊

One Saturday morning, I was walking home from the market with a shopping bag. I was looking forward to getting home and having a cold drink. As I turned a corner, I saw something that made me stop. 🔊

An elderly woman was struggling with a heavy shopping cart. The wheel had gotten stuck in a crack on the sidewalk. She was trying hard to pull it free, but she was not strong enough. She looked tired. Other people walked by, but no one stopped to help. 🔊

I did not think twice. I put down my bag and went over to her. "Let me help you, grandma," I said. I lifted the cart just enough to free the stuck wheel. Then I asked her where she was going. She lived about two blocks away, so I pushed the cart for her. She smiled and thanked me many times. 🔊

When we reached her home, she wanted to give me some fruit. I said no, but she insisted, so I took a small apple. Walking back, I felt a warm glow in my heart. Helping her took only ten minutes, but it made my whole day meaningful. A small act of kindness can make a big difference. 🔊

Everybody feels nervous sometimes. For me, the most nervous I have ever been was on the day of my middle school entrance exam. I had studied for months and done many practice questions. When the day came, my heart beat so fast I could feel it in my ears. 🔊

That morning I woke up very early. I checked my bag three times to make sure I had my admission ticket, pens, and pencils. At breakfast I could hardly eat. My mother told me to take deep breaths. "You have prepared well," she said. "Just go and do your best." Her words helped a little. 🔊

The exam room was very quiet. All the students sat at their desks with serious faces. When the teacher handed out the papers, my mind went blank for a few seconds. I closed my eyes, took a deep breath, and told myself to calm down. Slowly the words came back to me. I started writing, and the nervous feeling began to fade. 🔊

When the exam was over, I walked out feeling light and free. My parents were waiting for me outside. They did not ask me how I did. They just gave me a hug. I learned that feeling nervous is normal. What matters is not letting it stop you from doing what you need to do. 🔊

When I look back on my life, I see many things to be grateful for. First, I think of my family. My parents work very hard every day to give my sister and me a good life. They may not say "I love you" often, but their love shows in everything they do. 🔊

My father wakes up before the sun to go to work. Even when he is tired, he always asks about my day. My mother takes care of the house and still finds time to help me with my homework. My grandparents teach me old stories and values not found in any schoolbook. 🔊

I am also grateful for my teachers. They give their time and energy to help me learn and grow. When I struggle with a subject, they stay after class to explain it again. My friends, too, are a big part of my life. We share joys and sadness together. 🔊

Having a grateful heart does not mean my life is perfect. I have problems like anyone else. But when I focus on the good things I have instead of the things I do not have, I feel peaceful and strong. I want to remember this feeling and one day give back the same love and care to the people around me. 🔊
